mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic expansion, validating earnings upgrade cycles and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 55% of rolling 12-month windows, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerating below 1.0 percent annualized signals supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing demand, which compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of observations where INDPRO growth falls below 1.0%, or the signal exhibits no statistical significance after adjusting for seasonal factors.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings exceeding 220 trigger aggressive Federal Reserve tightening expectations, raising real interest rates and discounting future cash flows, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered periods, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% when tested against out-of-sample data from 2010-2014.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month additions to nonfarm payrolls exceeding 150,000 sustain aggregate demand and corporate earnings trajectories, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon without requiring additional macro triggers.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of consecutive months with payroll growth above 150k, or the rolling 3-year information coefficient falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential investment combined with accommodative short-term interest rates amplifies household balance sheet strength and consumption capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon through earnings revision momentum.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive risk-adjusted returns in more than 50% of rolling 24-month periods, or the interaction coefficient in a factor model is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts accelerating above 1.0 million annualized units during early economic recoveries signal renewed construction activity and consumer confidence, which positively impacts financial and industrial sectors and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1.0 million, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power beyond INDPRO growth.
